title: Plaid valued at $8B in employee share sale
description: The new valuation is a 31% increase from $6.1 billion Plaid reached in April.

title: Anthropic CEO stands firm as Pentagon deadline looms
description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said Thursday that he "cannot in good conscience accede" to the Pentagon's demands to give the military unrestricted access to its AI systems.

title: Google paid startup Form Energy $1B for its massive 100-hour battery
description: The deal paves the way for Form Energy to raise a new funding round before potentially going public next year.

title: So, we’re getting Prada Meta AI glasses, right?
description: Mark Zuckerberg was at Prada's fashion week event in Milan, leaving everyone to wonder if we're getting Meta AI glasses under the Prada brand.

title: Sophia Space raises $10M seed to demo novel space computers
description: The company's modular computer tiles offer a new vision for space data centers.

title: Mistral AI inks a deal with global consulting giant Accenture
description: Mistral AI lands a partnership with Accenture, the consultant that has also recently announced partnerships with rivals OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: Read AI launches an email-based ‘digital twin’ to help you with schedules and answers
description: Read AI is launching Ada, which can reply with your availability and extract answers from the company knowledge base and the web.

title: Bumble adds AI-powered photo feedback and profile guidance tools
description: Bumble and other popular dating apps, like Match Group's Tinder and Hinge, have all embraced AI-powered features.

title: Walmart agrees to $100M settlement over deceptive pay practices in Spark Driver program
description: The suit said Walmart would mislead drivers about their possible tips and would reduce their base pay, among other things.

title: Self-driving truck startup Einride raises $113M PIPE ahead of public debut
description: The proceeds will support Einride’s technology roadmap, global expansion, and autonomous deployments in North America, Europe, and the Middle East.

title: Cisco says hackers have been exploiting a critical bug to break into big customer networks since 2023
description: The U.S. government and its allies said hackers have been exploiting the newly identified bug in Cisco networking gear around the world for years, and urged organizations to patch.

title: Google launches Nano Banana 2 model with faster image generation
description: Google is making Nano Banana 2 a default model in Gemini app and in AI mode.

title: Microsoft’s Copilot Tasks AI uses its own computer to get things done
Jack Dorsey’s Block cuts nearly half of its staff in AI gamble
Why no magnets in Galaxy S26? Samsung R&D chief explains
